tweets.author: nextjs
tweets.text:

Add React <𝚅𝚒𝚎𝚠𝚃𝚛𝚊𝚗𝚜𝚒𝚝𝚒𝚘𝚗> animations to any React app. Also covers how Next.js can natively integrate them.

• Slide pages forward and back
• Smooth loading transitions
• Composition and accessibility handled

tweets.author: robinebers
tweets.text:
skill issue

plan mode in some harnesses (like cursor) does significantly more work before it comes to conclusions

don't believe anyone that claims things just because it works for them

peter is cool and he does a lot for this industry

but he also famously said he doesn't do PRs and code reviews

take everything everyone says with a huge grain of salt

especially when it's written in such an aggressive way

tweets.author: Rigario
tweets.text:
Many are running @NousResearch Hermes Agent now.
Here are some practical tips that help a lot, especially if you're coming from OpenClaw:

1. Nightly skill evolution is worth setting up. Link: https://t.co/qPs3QPIYgW
Pro tip: Add a second cronjob to evaluate the changes so you don't have to. Make sure it stops anything that tries to game the optimization loop.

2. Install Honcho if you're hitting memory issues. It gives proper cross-session recall, memory synthesis, and better long-term storage.

tweets.author: avthar
tweets.text:
Claude Code can self-improve over time. Here's how to set it up.

I built a custom /retro Agent Skill that runs after every feature is built:
-  It reviews git history, test results, and conversation context
- It suggests improvements to my CLAUDE.md, skills, slash commands, and other customizations.
- It also interviews me for my input to capture my own learnings in addition to Claude's.

I also recommend the Compound Engineering plugin from @every and the Claudeception skill from @blader for s

tweets.author: sentry
tweets.text:
🚨 You can now generate Sentry bug reports in one prompt!

Generate Sentry bug reports in these 3 steps:

1. Install the skill: npx skills add getsentry/sentry-for-ai --skill generate-bug-report
2. Spin up Claude Code in your project
3. Ask Claude: "Generate a bug report for me" 

Try it out for yourself and let us know what you think!

tweets.author: openservai
tweets.text:
How to add OpenServ skills: 

Step 1: Visit https://t.co/y7xbqnVPi4 and browse the available skills

Step 2: Run this command in your terminal:

npx skills add openserv-labs/skills

That's it. Your Claude Code client now has access to the full OpenServ skill library.

tweets.author: weareshiphero
tweets.text:
Giving your AI agent full read/write access to your ShipHero account is a bad idea. We just made the safe alternative dead simple.

ShipHero’s Public API Agents Skill gives Claude, Codex, or any MCP agent read-only access to your live warehouse data.

When your agent session ends, the skill includes an explicit revoke command to invalidate the token. This is important, so don’t skip this step. Short-lived tokens reduce the blast radius if a session log or context window is ever exposed.

Setup t

在 AI 的下一阶段，能留下的不是给现有产品加 AI 功能的平台，而是那些为 Agent 重新构建基础设施的。

Skill 从各处装了一堆，哪些安全、哪些能用，没人说得清。

Onchain OS Plugin Store 即将上线。任何人可以提交链上 Skill，经过 AI+人工安全审核后，一处安装。

提前体验👇
npx skills add okx/plugin-store

tweets.author: ChasePassi79437
tweets.text:
写了基于vercel的agent-browser cli和skill搭建的浏览器网页e2e-verifier codex custom agent，可以用来跑完任务后进行端到端验收，好处就是省功夫，不用compact或者new再去测（一般跑完一个开发任务context都不多了），在创建.codex/agents/e2e-verifier.toml，写入：
name = "e2e_verifier"
description = "Use agent-browser to perform end-to-end web verification for newly implemented features and output a structured test report."

model = "gpt-5.4"
model_reasoning_effort = "medium"

developer_instructions = """
You are an E2E verification subagent.

Your job is to accept a feature desc
